LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. OVERRULED AS TO 4TH THROUGH 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE FACE OF THE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT ESTABLISH THAT THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE TIME BARRED, AND PLAINTIFFS MET THE STANDARD FOR PLEADING DELAYED DISCOVERY. FURTHER, LIBERALLY CONSTRUING THE PLEADINGS, THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE ON DEMURRER THAT GOV. CODE 820.2 BARS THE CLAIMS. OVERRULED AS TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION, WHICH SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS ACTUAL FRAUD AND IS NOT BARRED BY GOV. CODE 822.2. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS RE CLAIMS BROUGHT BY PLAINTIFF STEPHEN C. ENGLER. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. --- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED - DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO PLAINTIFF STEPHEN ENGLER IS GRANTED. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT PLAINTIFF WAS 39 YEARS OLD AT ALL TIMES THAT THE PROMOTIONAL EXAMINATION WAS ADMINISTERED AND GAVE RISE TO THE ELIGIBILITY LIST FROM WHICH PROMOTIONS WERE MADE. ACCORDINGLY PLAINTIFF CANNOT STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR HIS CLAIMS OF AGE DISCRIMINATION OR FAILURE TO PREVENT DISCRIMINATION. FURTHERMORE, BECAUSE OF HIS INELIGIBILITY FOR PROMOTION DUE TO HIS FAILURE ON THE MAY 2008 PORTION OF THE EXAM, PLAINTIF'S DISCRIMINATION CLAIM ALSO FAILS. FINALLY, THE CONTINUING VIOLATION DOCTRINE DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE PLAINTIFF WAS NOT A MEMBER OF THE PROTECTED CLASS AT THE TIME OF THE INITIAL ALLEGED UNLAWFUL CONDUCT (THE EXAM). Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Demurrer To 4th Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ruth Bond, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3976, for defendant and moving party;
Murlene J. Randle, Esq. and R. William Reed, Esq., of the Law Offices of Murlene Randle, Tel. 415.352.0189, for plaintiffs.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Demurrer To 4th Amended Complaint is Sustained in part and overruled in part. Overruled as to 4th through 8th causes of action. The face of the Fourth Amended Complaint does not establish that these causes of action are time barred, and Plaintiffs met the standard for pleading delayed discovery. Further, liberally construing the pleadings, the Court cannot determine on demurrer that Gov. Code 820.2 bars the claims. Overruled as to the 6th cause of action, which sufficiently pleads actual fraud and is not barred by Gov. Code 822.2. Sustained with leave to amend as to the 7th cause of action for Plaintiff to plead a statutory basis for the injury. Sustained without leave to amend on the 9th through 12th causes of action; Plaintiff concedes these claims are barred by Worker's Compensation. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION To Compel The Disclosure Of Information Wrongly Withheld In An "Opt-Out" Procedure Ordered By The Court From All Captioned Defendants And For Sanctions - The court adopts the recommendation and rationale of Pro Tem Judge Huibonhoa as follows: Granted. The "opt-out" procedure agreed to by the parties specifies a 30-day return, and the notice mailed to the candidates makes clear on its face that "the Department will not disclose your information if" certain conditions are met, including returning the notice within 30 days. Those candidates who failed to comply with these terms did not timely or effectively opt-out, and their information thus is subject to disclosure. Monetary sanctions are denied. Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar May-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS RE CLAIMS BROUGHT BY PLAINTIFF STEPHEN C. ENGLER.

APPEARANCES: Murlene J. Randle, Esq. of Law Offices of Murlene J. Randle 352-0189 for pltfs & moving parties; Ruth M. Bond, Deputy City Atty 554-3976 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted - DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO PLAINTIFF STEPHEN ENGLER IS GRANTED. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT PLAINTIFF WAS 39 YEARS OLD AT ALL TIMES THAT THE PROMOTIONAL EXAMINATION WAS ADMINISTERED AND GAVE RISE TO THE ELIGIBILITY LIST FROM WHICH PROMOTIONS WERE MADE. ACCORDINGLY PLAINTIFF CANNOT STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR HIS CLAIMS OF AGE DISCRIMINATION OR FAILURE TO PREVENT DISCRIMINATION. FURTHERMORE, BECAUSE OF HIS INELIGIBILITY FOR PROMOTION DUE TO HIS FAILURE ON THE MAY 2008 PORTION OF THE EXAM, PLAINTIF'S DISCRIMINATION CLAIM ALSO FAILS. FINALLY, THE CONTINUING VIOLATION DOCTRINE DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE PLAINTIFF WAS NOT A MEMBER OF THE PROTECTED CLASS AT THE TIME OF THE INITIAL ALLEGED UNLAWFUL CONDUCT (THE EXAM).
Defendant's order granting motion for judgment on the pleadings signed. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
